validations_not_run: npm test (frontend Karma requires Chromium; not executed)
npm run frisby (server-side API integration; requires SCA branch deps to install cleanly first)

npm run rsn (Refactoring Safety Net WILL fail by design because this branch deliberately rewrites challenge-relevant files — reviewers must run `npm run rsn:update` after merge to refresh the snapshot)
tsc compile (deferred until SCA branch deps are installed — see sequencing notes)

sequencing_notes: lib/insecurity.ts and routes/login.ts/changePassword.ts on this branch import express-jwt v8 named export `expressjwt` and use jsonwebtoken v9 callback signatures. Those packages are bumped on the companion branch remediation/sca-20260512-022728. Merge the SCA PR BEFORE (or together with) this SAST PR so that `npm install` produces a buildable tree.

vulnerable_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = '${req.body.email || ''}' AND password = '${security.hash(req.body.password || '')}' AND deletedAt IS NULL`, { model: UserModel, plain: true })

changed_code.snippet: UserModel.findOne({ where: { email, deletedAt: null } }).then((authenticatedUser) => { if (!authenticatedUser || !security.verifyPassword(password, authenticatedUser.password)) { res.status(401).send(res.__('Invalid email or password.')); return } ... })

fixes.fix_rationale: Switch from string-template SQL to a parameterised Sequelize model query; verify the password with scrypt+timingSafeEqual via security.verifyPassword. No user-controlled string is concatenated into SQL, so tautology bypass (`' OR 1=1--`) is no longer possible.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Existing seeded users' MD5-hashed passwords still verify via the legacy fallback in security.verifyPassword. New writes (registration on server.ts and changePassword.ts) hash via scrypt. Login flow, TOTP branch, and afterLogin/basket creation are unchanged.

validation.notes: API integration: reviewers must run npm run frisby (test/api/loginApiSpec.ts).

fixes.residual_risk: Legacy MD5 rows remain crackable until users re-log in and we transparently rehash (extension already supported via verifyPassword). Recommend a one-shot migration on next maintenance window.

vulnerable_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query(`SELECT * FROM Products WHERE ((name LIKE '%${criteria}%' OR description LIKE '%${criteria}%') AND deletedAt IS NULL) ORDER BY name`)

changed_code.snippet: ProductModel.findAll({ where: { [Op.and]: [{ deletedAt: null }, { [Op.or]: [{ name: { [Op.like]: likePattern } }, { description: { [Op.like]: likePattern } }] }] }, order: [['name','ASC']] })

fixes.fix_rationale: Use Sequelize's parameterised model query with Op.like. The driver escapes the criteria value so UNION SELECT / sqlite_master extraction is no longer possible.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Identical product list semantics; LIKE pattern includes leading/trailing % wildcards as before. The 200-char truncation is retained.
fixes.residual_risk: None for SQLi. The unionSqlInjectionChallenge / dbSchemaChallenge solve paths intentionally removed; this is correct.

vulnerable_code.snippet: const privateKey = '-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----...committed in source...'

changed_code.snippet: const privateKey = process.env.JWT_PRIVATE_KEY ?? readKey(process.env.JWT_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH ?? 'encryptionkeys/jwt.key')

fixes.fix_rationale: Hard-coded RSA private key is fully removed from source. Key material is loaded from env or from a gitignored file. .gitignore now excludes encryptionkeys/*.key and ftp/*.bak/*.kdbx/*.pyc. .env.example documents the required variables and rotation steps.
fixes.functionality_preservation: JWT issuance and verification continue to use RS256. Existing tokens signed by the leaked key are NO LONGER ACCEPTED because the new private key must be regenerated — this is the intended consequence of treating the old key as compromised. Operators must rotate before deploy.

validation.notes: DEVELOPER ACTION REQUIRED: generate a fresh keypair and a SECURITY_ANSWER_HMAC_SECRET before booting the app.

fixes.residual_risk: git history still contains the leaked key. A history rewrite (git filter-repo or BFG) is required to truly purge it. Tracked separately as a developer_decision_required item below.

changed_code.snippet: export const hash = (data) => `${salt}$${scryptSync(data, salt, 64, { N: 16384 }).toString('hex')}`; verifyPassword(plain, stored) uses crypto.timingSafeEqual; legacyHash retained for one-shot upgrade of existing rows.

fixes.fix_rationale: Password hashing now uses scrypt with a per-credential random salt and constant-time verification. legacyHash() is retained ONLY so existing MD5 rows verify once and can be silently upgraded on next login (handled inside verifyPassword).
fixes.functionality_preservation: Login flow continues to work for seeded MD5 rows via the legacy fallback. New password writes (registration, change-password) produce scrypt hashes.
fixes.residual_risk: Until all users log in once, their rows remain MD5. Recommend an opportunistic migration job.

changed_code.snippet: const hmacSecret = process.env.SECURITY_ANSWER_HMAC_SECRET ?? (privateKey || 'change-me-in-env'); crypto.createHmac('sha256', hmacSecret)

fixes.fix_rationale: HMAC secret is now loaded from SECURITY_ANSWER_HMAC_SECRET environment variable. The hard-coded literal is fully removed.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Security-answer reset flow continues to work once the env var is set. Pre-existing SecurityAnswers rows (HMACed with the old leaked literal) will no longer validate — a one-shot reseed of the test fixtures is necessary.

validation.notes: Reviewer: rotate the secret in production and reseed dev data.

fixes.residual_risk: Existing SecurityAnswer rows must be reset. Documented in .env.example.

vulnerable_code.snippet: export const denyAll = () => expressJwt({ secret: '' + Math.random() } as any)

changed_code.snippet: export const denyAll = () => (_req, res, _next) => { res.status(403).json({ error: 'Forbidden' }) }

fixes.fix_rationale: denyAll is now an explicit 403 responder; authorization decisions never depend on JWT signature verification with a random secret (which, combined with the alg:none bug in express-jwt 0.1.3, was bypassable).
fixes.functionality_preservation: Every mount point that previously denied access still denies access. The HTTP status changes from 401 to 403, which is the semantically correct code for an explicit deny.

validation.notes: Reviewers: confirm denyAll'd endpoints still return non-200 in frisby.

changed_code.snippet: isRedirectAllowed parses the URL and compares protocol/host/pathname against each entry's parsed URL — no substring match.

fixes.fix_rationale: Substring matching is replaced with parsed-URL origin + path-prefix comparison. Open-redirect via `?to=https://evil.com/?x=https://github.com/juice-shop/juice-shop` no longer succeeds.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Every URL in the allow-list still resolves to an allowed redirect. Adding new destinations now requires explicit listing of the full origin.

validation.notes: Reviewer: spot-check /redirect with each allowlisted URL.

changed_code.snippet: const username = entities.encode(user.username ?? '');  CSP = "img-src 'self' data:; script-src 'self'; default-src 'self'"

fixes.fix_rationale: Removed the eval() entirely; username is HTML-escaped before injection into the Pug template. CSP is now built from a static string with no user-controlled interpolation; the previous `unsafe-eval` directive is also dropped.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Profile page still renders the user's username; the #{...} "feature" (which was solely the SSTI vector) is removed. CSP becomes stricter, which may break inline analytics that relied on unsafe-eval — but unsafe-eval should never have been there.
fixes.residual_risk: None for RCE/XSS through this path.

changed_code.snippet: isValidOrderLine(line) checks productId, quantity, customerReference, couponCode types; non-object payload returns 400. No code evaluation.

fixes.fix_rationale: The notevil/vm-based eval is removed entirely. The endpoint now accepts a strict JSON schema (array of typed order lines, max 100). notevil's documented sandbox escapes and infinite-loop DoS are eliminated by design.
fixes.functionality_preservation: B2B clients sending structured JSON continue to be served. Clients that depended on submitting JavaScript expressions will need to switch to plain JSON — that capability was a vulnerability, not a feature.

validation.notes: Reviewer: confirm B2B clients can still place orders with structured payloads.

changed_code.snippet: Sanitise filename via path.basename; resolve against fixed EXTRACT_ROOT; require startsWith(EXTRACT_ROOT + sep); reject symlinks/absolute/null-byte; enforce per-entry 1MiB + total 5MiB caps.

fixes.fix_rationale: Replaces the substring check with proper extract-root containment. Symlink overwrite, traversal, and zip-bomb DoS are all blocked.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Legitimate complaint zip uploads with filenames at the top level still extract to uploads/complaints. The fileWriteChallenge solve target (writing to ftp/legal.md) is removed — that was the vulnerability.

vulnerable_code.snippet: libxml.parseXml(data, { noblanks: true, noent: true, nocdata: true })

changed_code.snippet: libxml.parseXml(data, { noblanks: true, noent: false, nocdata: true, dtdload: false, dtdvalid: false, nonet: true })

fixes.fix_rationale: Disable entity expansion (noent:false), DTD load (dtdload:false), and network access (nonet:true). Closes XXE file-disclosure and billion-laughs DoS.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Well-formed customer-complaint XML still parses (the legacy interface returns 410 by design).

changed_code.snippet: yaml.load(data, { schema: yaml.FAILSAFE_SCHEMA, json: false }); 50KB input cap

fixes.fix_rationale: FAILSAFE_SCHEMA restricts decoded types to strings/maps/sequences only — no JS object instantiation; combined with a 50KB input cap to bound anchor amplification.
fixes.functionality_preservation: YAML config uploads still parse as nested map/array/string structures.

vulnerable_code.snippet: app.post('/api/Users', verify.registerAdminChallenge())  // finale auto-CRUD accepts arbitrary fields

changed_code.snippet: Strip every field not on the registration whitelist (email,password,passwordRepeat,username,profileImage,securityQuestion,securityAnswer); force role='customer'; hash password with scrypt before finale persists.

fixes.fix_rationale: Hard-coded role + field whitelist makes it impossible for a client to register as admin/accounting/deluxe. Password is pre-hashed so finale never stores plaintext.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Normal registration unchanged; client-supplied role/deluxeToken/lastLoginIp are now silently dropped — which is the intended behaviour.
fixes.residual_risk: None for mass-assignment on this route. Finale-issued PUT/PATCH on other models still warrants review (see JS-AUDIT-015).

vulnerable_code.snippet: app.use('/ftp', serveIndexMiddleware, serveIndex('ftp', ...)); app.use('/encryptionkeys', serveIndexMiddleware, serveIndex('encryptionkeys', ...)); app.use('/support/logs', serveIndexMiddleware, serveIndex('logs', ...))

changed_code.snippet: All three directory-listing mounts removed. Sensitive files (encryptionkeys/premium.key, ftp/*.bak, ftp/incident-support.kdbx, ftp/encrypt.pyc, ftp/suspicious_errors.yml) removed from the repo. .gitignore extended to keep them out.

fixes.fix_rationale: Combined: no listing, no file servers, no files on disk to be served. The JWT public key (encryptionkeys/jwt.pub) is retained — it is a public key and is intentionally available.
fixes.functionality_preservation: The CTF-mode 'discover the kdbx' challenge is broken by this change because the file is gone. Acceptable per engagement rules (treat as production).

validation.notes: DEVELOPER ACTION: git history rewrite (git filter-repo or BFG) is required to permanently purge the previously-committed secrets. Tracked separately.

fixes.residual_risk: Secrets remain in git history until a history rewrite is performed.

vulnerable_code.snippet: fetch(url) with no scheme/host/DNS check; on failure stored the raw url as profileImage

changed_code.snippet: Require https scheme (configurable); resolve DNS and reject RFC1918/loopback/link-local/multicast; 5MiB cap + 5s timeout; reject non-image Content-Type; NEVER persist the raw URL on failure.

fixes.fix_rationale: Full SSRF guard plus removal of the stored-URL fallback that chained with the CSP-injection XSS in JS-AUDIT-009.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Users uploading a public https image URL still get a stored profile image.
fixes.residual_risk: DNS rebinding remains an edge case; recommend repeating the resolve at fetch-time via a pinned IP outbound proxy in production.

changed_code.snippet: Validate id against /^[\w-]{1,60}$/; query with equality predicate { orderId: rawId } — no $where

fixes.fix_rationale: MarsDB's $where executes via new Function(); replacing it with an equality predicate removes the JS code-injection sink. Strict regex validation gives defence-in-depth.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Looking up an order by orderId continues to work.

changed_code.snippet: Number(req.params.id) must be a positive integer; db.reviewsCollection.find({ product: id })

fixes.fix_rationale: Strict integer coercion + equality predicate replaces the $where injection. The global busy-loop sleep() helper is also removed.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Reviews are still listed per product.

vulnerable_code.snippet: db.reviewsCollection.update({ _id: req.body.id }, { $set: { message: req.body.message } }, { multi: true })

changed_code.snippet: Filter { _id: stringId, author: user.data.email }; drop multi:true; require non-empty id and message<=2000 chars

fixes.fix_rationale: Scopes the update to the caller's own reviews and drops multi:true, preventing operator-injection (`{$ne:null}`) from overwriting every review.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Users can still edit their own reviews.

changed_code.snippet: appendUserId now DELETES any client-supplied req.body.UserId BEFORE inserting the trusted one from the verified JWT.

fixes.fix_rationale: Hardening the middleware fixes every downstream IDOR that takes UserId from req.body, including wallet read/write.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Routes that legitimately need UserId still receive it; the value is just no longer attacker-controllable.

vulnerable_code.snippet: memories = await MemoryModel.findAll({ where: { UserId: req.body.UserId } })

changed_code.snippet: MemoryModel.findAll({ where: { UserId: loggedInUser.data.id } })

fixes.fix_rationale: Bind the query directly to the verified-JWT user id; also covered defensively by the hardened appendUserId middleware.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Data export returns the caller's own memories.

changed_code.snippet: userIdFromRequest(req) reads from security.authenticatedUsers; all three handlers use that id, validate the path param is an integer, and return 401/400/404 appropriately.

fixes.fix_rationale: Server-derived user id; no acceptance of client-controlled UserId on this route.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Address listing / fetch / delete continue to operate for the authenticated owner.

vulnerable_code.snippet: UserModel.findOne({ where: { id: req.body.UserId, role: 'customer' } }); paymentMode branches optional → free upgrade

changed_code.snippet: Require explicit paymentMode in {wallet,card}; derive userId from JWT; verify funds/card before issuing the role change.

fixes.fix_rationale: Closes both the IDOR (body UserId) and the free-upgrade hole (missing paymentMode).
fixes.functionality_preservation: Legitimate deluxe upgrades via wallet or card still succeed; explicit error for missing paymentMode.

vulnerable_code.snippet: GET /rest/user/change-password with passwords in req.query; current password check skipped if absent

changed_code.snippet: POST /rest/user/change-password; currentPassword always required and verified via constant-time scrypt; Authorization Bearer required (no cookie auth); newPassword stored as scrypt hash with min length 10.

fixes.fix_rationale: Removes the CSRF surface (cookie-auth GET), the query-string credential leak, and the optional-current-password bypass.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Front-end must POST to /rest/user/change-password with a Bearer token. The UI already constructs Authorization headers for other authenticated calls, so this is a small adjustment.

validation.notes: Reviewer: confirm the Angular client posts with Bearer token + JSON body.

fixes.residual_risk: Front-end may need a one-line tweak; covered by the test suite changes reviewers should run.

changed_code.snippet: app.set('trust proxy', TRUSTED_PROXIES env list); rateLimit keyGenerator: req => req.ip

fixes.fix_rationale: Only trust proxies listed in TRUSTED_PROXIES; key the limit off req.ip after the trust-proxy hop so attackers cannot rotate X-Forwarded-For.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Legitimate users hitting reset-password are still rate-limited; behind a real proxy chain the limiter remains effective.
fixes.residual_risk: Low. Recommend adding CAPTCHA/account-level lockout for full protection.

vulnerable_code.snippet: res.json({ config })  // dumps entire node-config object; no auth

changed_code.snippet: Whitelisted public config keys only; mount gated by security.isAuthorized().

fixes.fix_rationale: Combined auth gate plus key whitelist eliminates secret leakage.
fixes.functionality_preservation: SPA still receives every config key it needs for rendering (logo, theme, etc.).

changed_code.snippet: Coerce id with Number(); require Number.isInteger(id) && id>0; pass plain integer to Sequelize.

fixes.fix_rationale: JSON.parse on a path parameter was the operator-injection sink. Integer coercion makes Sequelize operator objects impossible.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Looking up a single recycle item still works.

changed_code.snippet: Reject URL trainingFile at startup; whitelist handler names against ['productPrice','couponCode','functionTest']

fixes.fix_rationale: Removes the unauthenticated remote-fetch supply-chain hole and closes property injection on bot handler dispatch.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Bot continues to greet, answer FAQs, look up product prices and coupons via the bundled training data.
fixes.residual_risk: Add new handler names to the whitelist if/when the bundled training set introduces new functions.

changed_code.snippet: Load from process.env.ALCHEMY_WSS_URL; 503 if unset

fixes.fix_rationale: Removes the hard-coded API key. The leaked key must be rotated.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Web3 listener routes continue to work once the env var is provided.
fixes.residual_risk: Rotation must be performed by ops.

changed_code.snippet: Explicit origin allow-list (CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS env); credentials:false; method/header whitelist.

fixes.fix_rationale: Restricting Access-Control-Allow-Origin to an explicit list reinstates same-origin protection for the API.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Local development at http://localhost:3000 / :4200 is allowed by default; production deployments must set CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS.

changed_code.snippet: Slash-collapse middleware removed; URL normalisation is the responsibility of the reverse proxy.

fixes.fix_rationale: Removes the routing-desync vector that helped substring-based bypasses on redirect/file-server allow-lists.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Reverse proxy / CDN deployments normally already normalise paths.

changed_code.snippet: errorhandler() only mounted when NODE_ENV==='development'; in production a JSON responder hides stack frames

fixes.fix_rationale: Stack traces and source paths are no longer exposed to internet callers.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Local dev experience unchanged.

changed_code.snippet: Require Bearer METRICS_BEARER_TOKEN; 404 when unset, 401 on bad token.

fixes.fix_rationale: Prevents unauthenticated /metrics scraping. Operators bind on an internal listener in production.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Metrics still scrape-able with the configured token.

changed_code.snippet: Strict ALLOWED_FILES set (['jwt.pub']); resolve target and verify startsWith(KEY_ROOT + path.sep).

fixes.fix_rationale: Explicit allow-list defeats URL-encoded traversal, null-byte truncation, and OS-specific separators.
fixes.functionality_preservation: JWT public key is still available; premium.key has been removed from the repo (see JS-AUDIT-016).

changed_code.snippet: Always render the fixed 'dataErasureResult' view; only the email + securityAnswer fields are passed in; ...req.body spread removed.

fixes.fix_rationale: Removes the LFR/template-object-injection path.
fixes.functionality_preservation: The dataerasure confirmation page still renders for legitimate users.

vulnerable_code.snippet: cardNum: { type: DataTypes.INTEGER, validate: { isInt: true, min: 1e15, max: 1e16-2 } }

changed_code.snippet: Sequelize beforeValidate hook truncates cardNum to last 4 digits; nullable paymentToken column added for PCI-compliant tokenisation reference.

fixes.fix_rationale: Database never persists more than the last 4 digits, even if the route accepts a full PAN. Aligns with PCI-DSS scope-reduction guidance.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Frontend and payment.ts already mask all but the last 4 digits in responses; the existing API contract is preserved. Real charging should be delegated to a processor via paymentToken.

validation.notes: Reviewer should run npm run frisby (paymentApiSpec).

fixes.residual_risk: Legacy rows containing full PANs must be purged from production databases — not a code change.

vulnerable_code.snippet: const mnemonic = 'purpose betray marriage blame ...'; if (req.body.privateKey === privateKey) {...}

changed_code.snippet: Load mnemonic from NFT_CHALLENGE_MNEMONIC env var; compare keys via crypto.timingSafeEqual

fixes.fix_rationale: Removes the hard-coded HD-wallet mnemonic and the timing-leaking equality check.
fixes.functionality_preservation: NFT challenge continues to work once the env var is configured.
fixes.residual_risk: Operator must generate and configure a new mnemonic.

vulnerable_code.snippet: for (const field of requestedFields) { baseUser[field] = user?.data[field] }

changed_code.snippet: Fixed PUBLIC_FIELDS = ['id','email','lastLoginIp','profileImage']; ?fields= query is ignored.

fixes.fix_rationale: Eliminates client-controlled field projection — password hash and totpSecret cannot be requested.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Front-end consumers receive the same standard fields; the optional ?fields= query was only ever a hazard.

vulnerable_code.snippet: for (const { name, exclude, model } of autoModels) { finale.resource({ model, endpoints: [...], excludeAttributes: exclude }) }

changed_code.snippet: (unchanged)

fixes.fix_rationale: Replacing finale-rest auto-CRUD with explicit hand-written routes across 14 models is a multi-day effort that touches the entire API surface plus the frontend SDKs and the Frisby/Cypress specs. JS-AUDIT-014 (admin role mass-assignment) — the most acute consequence — is closed by the registration-route hardening. Per-model attribute authorisation is still recommended but should be planned as a focused refactor.
fixes.functionality_preservation: Out of scope for this PR.
fixes.residual_risk: HIGH until full migration is done. As a stop-gap, JS-AUDIT-014 hardening prevents the most severe exploit (admin registration).

vulnerable_code.snippet: if ((await fs.lstat(currPath)).isDirectory()) { ... } else { const code = await fs.readFile(currPath, 'utf8') ... }

changed_code.snippet: (unchanged)

fixes.fix_rationale: Listed `needs_review` in the canonical findings. Path is internal-only (startup-time scan of the repo source tree on a trusted FS). Real-world TOCTOU window is negligible. Not fixing avoids unnecessary churn in challenge-detection code.
fixes.functionality_preservation: n/a

validation.notes: Trivial fix: replace lstat+readFile with try/catch around readFile (EISDIR). Track separately if reviewers prefer to close it.

changed_code.snippet: (unchanged)

fixes.fix_rationale: `needs_review` finding; field is internal not user input. The publicly-served /support/logs vector is closed by JS-AUDIT-016.
fixes.functionality_preservation: n/a

validation.notes: Recommend JSON log encoder; tracked separately.

vulnerable_code.snippet: ftp/incident-support.kdbx, ftp/*.bak, encryptionkeys/premium.key, lib/insecurity.ts hard-coded private key, routes/web3Wallet.ts and nftMint.ts hard-coded Alchemy key, routes/checkKeys.ts hard-coded mnemonic

fixes.fix_rationale: Working-tree fixes remove these from HEAD but they remain reachable in earlier commits. A `git filter-repo` (or BFG Repo-Cleaner) pass followed by a forced rotation of every secret is required to fully remediate.
fixes.functionality_preservation: History rewrite invalidates existing checkouts/forks; coordinate with all collaborators.
fixes.residual_risk: HIGH until performed; the JWT key, HMAC secret, Alchemy key, and HD mnemonic must be treated as fully compromised.

changed_code.snippet: (the entire block is removed as part of the SSRF rewrite)

fixes.fix_rationale: Marked false_positive in findings.json; the regex no longer exists after the JS-AUDIT-017 rewrite.
fixes.functionality_preservation: n/a
